meaning: adj.光滑的,熟练的,聪明的,华而不实的,陈腐的,平凡的,老套的adv.灵活地,聪明地vt.使光滑,使漂亮n.平滑的水面,平滑器,修光工具vi.打扮整洁
examples: They are also covered with a slick, transparent lid that reduces drag.
Boat wakes appear as slightly curved lines, accentuated by the pastel tones of the oil slick.
The roads were slick with rain.
That is, slick, confident and sales driven, and more committed to style than substance.
Hertz's e-bikes come in two slick-looking models, powered by lithium ion batteries and nearly silent motors.
